The rail portfolio has come into stark relief in recent months with the
spike in oil prices, just as it did in the 1970s.
Rail freight and passenger rail proves its worth when there is a
price crunch.
In financial terms, Kiwi Rail is on track to deliver its
160 million earnings target this financial year by June 30,
2026. That is up from last year when it exceeded its
110 million earnings target.
Interislander reliability is at 98%,
Air NZ must look on with envy of that result.
Of note, in 2021, it was 81% reliable, then
reliable in 2022, and has picked up steadily since then.
This is due to a substantial increase in maintenance investment and great work by
crews and management. Likewise, rail freight service
reliability is up too. That matters because turning up on
time is what matters in freight. That is why rail freight volumes are
up 7% this year versus road freight increasing by
2%. You're witnessing market share shifting to rail.
We want to put on record our thanks to Ms.
Tindall, her board, and the whole team at Kiwi Rail because we are
seeing a firm, disciplined focus on performance.
Railway workers will look back on this period in their history as the turning point
for long-term success.

McKenzie, his board, and the whole team at Ferry Holdings.

commercial set of arrangements,
sensible, affordable port fees.
The ferry operator,
the secure future for freighters and families on the Cook Strait, all

IRX program. This is a project that all parliamentarians should

control, to drop the magpie attraction to shiny designs,
and appoint competent men and women to deliver only what is needed to do the job.

infrastructure projects. It placed huge demand on the small construction sector,
and prices then shot up. The blowout there was an inside job.
Instead, we need to have an agreed infrastructure pipeline, spend
more than 60 cents on every dollar on maintenance and renewal,
and sequence major projects in a steady manner that drives supply through
stable demand.
That is what we are doing in rail already.
Some politicians have ambitions for sainthood when they just need to attend
confession.
